task_completion.description: Verify that the legacy stylesheets preference is enabled in Tor Browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot of Tor Browser navigated to 'about:config' showing the preference 'toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets' set to 'true'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a 'chrome' directory within the Tor Browser profile directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or terminal screenshot showing a newly created folder named exactly 'chrome' located inside the Tor Browser's active profile directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'userChrome.css' file inside the 'chrome' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or terminal screenshot showing a file named exactly 'userChrome.css' inside the 'chrome' folder.

task_completion.description: Verify the CSS file contains the required rules to hide the new tab and unified extensions buttons.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor screenshot showing the 'userChrome.css' file containing '#tabs-newtab-button { display: none !important; }' and '#unified-extensions-button { display: none !important; }'.

task_completion.description: Verify the CSS file contains the required rule to style the active tab background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor screenshot showing the 'userChrome.css' file containing '.tab-background[selected="true"] { background-color: #ffaa00 !important; }'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Tor Browser application to change preferences.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Tor Browser GUI open at 'about:config', rather than the agent just appending text to a 'prefs.js' file blindly via terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the 'chrome' folder was created in the genuine Tor Browser profile directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the file path in the file manager or terminal to ensure it is a legitimate Tor Browser profile path (e.g., containing 'TorBrowser/Data/Browser/profile.default') and not a dummy directory.

integrity.description: Verify the CSS content was actually saved into the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of a text editor saving the file or a terminal command (like 'cat') outputting the contents of the actual 'userChrome.css' file, rather than just printing the text to the console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: To successfully complete this task, the agent must set the preference 'toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets' to true. Furthermore, Firefox/Tor architecture requires the agent to create a folder named exactly 'chrome' inside the active Tor Browser profile directory, and within that folder, create a file named exactly 'userChrome.css' containing the specified CSS rules.
